mainEntity.name: How does schema markup (like LocalBusiness or FAQ) actually help with "near me" voice search?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Use structured data like LocalBusiness and FAQ schema to tell search engines exactly who you are, where you are, and which questions you answer—this makes it easier for voice assistants to trust and quote you.

Schema doesn’t replace good content or listings, but it strengthens them by adding machine‑readable context.

Key factors:
- LocalBusiness schema: name, address, phone, geo coordinates, hours, and more.
- FAQ schema: real questions and concise answers marked up in a dedicated FAQ section.
- Clean implementation: no errors or misleading fields; validate regularly.
- Placement: include LocalBusiness on key pages, FAQ schema on Q&A blocks.

If you’re non‑technical, start with reputable plugins or generators and test your markup. Done carefully, schema is one of the highest‑leverage technical steps for “near me” voice optimization.
acceptedAnswer.description: LocalBusiness and FAQ schema give search engines structured data about your location, contact info, hours, and key Q&A, making it easier for voice assistants to understand and confidently quote your business in “near me” answers. It amplifies good content and listings rather than replacing them.
